name: First Community Bank
description_short: First Community Bank Bluefield, VA — FDIC-insured community bank offering digital banking, ATM access, personal and business accounts, mortgages, and we...
description_long: First Community Bank is a traditional community bank that operates physical branches, including locations in Richmond and Pineville. The bank recently relocated its Pineville branch to 243 Main Avenue and maintains multiple service centers throughout its service area. As an FDIC-insured institution, deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to applicable limits. First Community Bank offers a comprehensive range of banking products and services including personal checking and savings accounts, business banking solutions, mortgage lending (home purchase and refinance), trust accounts, credit card accounts, and wealth management services. The bank provides digital banking capabilities through online platforms and a mobile app called Xpress by FCB, enabling customers to manage accounts 24/7. Additional services include remote deposit capture, ATM access through their XpressBanker network, loan payment processing, and check reordering. The institution also offers preferred customer programs and perks for qualifying customers. First Community Bank distinguishes itself as a community-focused institution with physical branch locations and personalized service. The bank emphasizes accessibility through multiple banking channels—both in-person at branches and remotely through digital platforms. Their marketing emphasizes guiding customers' financial journeys and maintaining service availability that accommodates modern schedules. The recent branch relocation and expansion of digital services suggest ongoing investment in customer accessibility. As a traditional community bank, First Community Bank serves customers seeking full-service banking with both digital convenience and local branch access. The website content focuses on account opening and service availability rather than competitive rates or detailed product terms, which limits transparency around pricing, APYs, and loan rates. Prospective customers should contact the bank directly for specific rate and fee information before opening accounts.
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with U.S. Government backing
24/7 digital banking and mobile app (Xpress by FCB) for remote account management
Multiple service channels: online banking, mobile app, physical branches, and ATM network (XpressBanker)
Comprehensive product range including personal, business, mortgage, trust, and wealth management services
Free digital banking services available to customers
Recent branch expansion and relocation showing commitment to customer accessibility
Remote deposit capture capability for convenient check deposits
cons: Website does not disclose specific interest rates, APYs, or APRs for any products
Limited fee information available on the public website
No information about minimum balance requirements or account opening processes
Geographic service area not clearly defined on the homepage
Minimal detail about loan terms, mortgage options, or credit card features
best_for: Local customers in Richmond and Pineville seeking traditional community bank services with branch access
Customers wanting both digital banking convenience and in-person service options
Small business owners needing comprehensive business banking solutions
Customers prioritizing FDIC insurance and established bank stability
diagnosis: First Community Bank is best for customers in its service areas (Richmond and Pineville) who value the combination of traditional community banking with modern digital convenience and FDIC insurance. Prospective customers should contact the bank directly or visit in person to obtain specific information about rates, fees, and terms, as these details are not disclosed on the public website.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
